say: Hi! I'm Pathfinder -- your companion here. Good to meet you!

sayByAnswer.fallback: Hi! I'm Pathfinder -- your companion here. Good to meet you!

map.CSE: Hi! I'm Pathfinder. I already pulled your details -- CSE, nice! You're sitting right in the middle of the tech world. Let's figure out where YOU fit in it.
map.ECE: Hi! I'm Pathfinder. I checked your details -- ECE! Chips, circuits and signals run the world, and I've got a plan shaped just for your branch.
map.Civil: Hi! I'm Pathfinder. Your details say Civil -- the people who literally build the world. Let's build your career the same way, brick by brick.
map.EEE: Hi! I'm Pathfinder. I see you're EEE -- power, machines, control systems. Big field, big opportunities. Let's map yours out.
map.Mechanical: Hi! I'm Pathfinder. Details say Mechanical -- the OG engineering branch. Design, robotics, manufacturing... lots of doors. Let's find yours.

say: Before the serious stuff -- what do you actually enjoy when you're NOT studying? Games, sport, music, drawing, anything goes.
Love that. Now the big one -- when you picture life a few years out, what's the dream? Doesn't have to be polished, just say it how it feels.
So when you picture yourself after college -- which of these feels most like you?
Founder energy! Forget salary packages for a second -- when you picture your startup working, what's the dream scale?
Okay, honest question -- what package are you dreaming of?

sayByAnswer.fallback: Okay, honest question -- what package are you dreaming of?

map.Big enterprise (FAANG-style): Big league, I like it. Honest question then -- what's the package you're dreaming of?
map.Solid job, good growth: Respect -- stability plus growth is a real strategy. So what package are you aiming at?
map.My own startup: Founder energy! Even founders benchmark themselves though -- what income level are you aiming for?

say: Then let's be real with each other -- goals like that don't happen by attending classes alone.

sayByAnswer.fallback: Then let's be real with each other -- goals like that don't happen by attending classes alone.

map.Above 50 LPA: 50+ LPA -- I won't sugarcoat it: that's top 1% territory. It means consistent daily practice, real projects, and showing up even when it's boring. The good news? Starting in year 1, you have TIME on your side. Ready to hear how?
map.Below 50 LPA: Totally achievable -- but 'achievable' still means real, consistent work: skills, projects, practice. Most people underestimate that part. The good news is you're starting early, and that's the biggest advantage there is. Want the plan?

say: Cool. Which field are you most into right now?
What draws you to that field the most?

sayByAnswer.fallback: What draws you to that field the most?

map.AI/ML: Are you more curious about how AI models actually learn, or more into the cool stuff you can build with them?
map.Cybersecurity: Is it the hacker/puzzle-solving side that pulls you in, or more the idea of protecting systems and data?
map.IoT: Are you more into the physical gadgets and sensors side, or the idea of connecting everyday things to the internet?
map.ECE: Are you more into the hardware/circuits side, or the signal-processing/software side?
map.EEE: Are you leaning more toward power systems, or control systems and automation?
map.Web Dev: Frontend, backend, or do you like doing both end to end?
map.Cloud/DevOps: Is it the idea of running apps at massive scale that excites you, or more the automation/DevOps side of things?
map.Mobile Dev: iOS, Android, or cross-platform stuff like Flutter or React Native?
map.Blockchain: Is it the decentralization idea that hooks you, or more the tech behind smart contracts and crypto?
map.Data Science: Do you enjoy the analysis and stats side more, or the visualization and storytelling side?
map.Marketing: Are you more drawn to the creative side -- brand, content, campaigns -- or the numbers side like growth and analytics?
map.Finance: Is it markets and investing that pull you in, or more the corporate side -- valuation, deals, planning?
map.HR: Are you more into the people side -- hiring, culture -- or the strategy side like org design and people analytics?
map.Operations: Do you like the supply-chain and logistics puzzle, or more the process-improvement and efficiency side?
map.Business Analytics: Do you enjoy digging into data to find the story, or presenting insights that change decisions?
map.VLSI Design: Is it designing the chips themselves that excites you, or verifying they actually work?
map.Embedded Systems: Are you more into programming the tiny brains inside devices, or wiring up the hardware around them?
map.Signal Processing: Audio, images, or communications -- which kind of signal sounds most fun to play with?
map.Power Systems: Grids and renewables, or the machines side -- motors, drives, transformers?
map.Control Systems: Is it automation and PLCs that pull you, or the math of making systems behave?
map.Structural Design: Bridges and towers, or buildings -- what would you love to see standing because of you?
map.Construction Management: Is it running big sites and teams that appeals, or the planning/scheduling puzzle?
map.GIS & Surveying: Maps and drones, or the data side -- turning terrain into decisions?
map.CAD & Design: Product design and modeling, or simulation -- stress-testing things before they exist?
map.Robotics: Building the robot body, or programming its brain -- which half grabs you more?
map.Thermal & Energy: Engines and turbines, or the green side -- solar, batteries, sustainable energy?
map.Manufacturing & 3D Printing: Traditional production lines, or the new world of additive manufacturing and 3D printing?

say: Honestly now -- how many hours a week can you actually give this outside classes?
When something new actually clicks for you, how does it usually happen?
Last one -- how settled do you feel about {answers.domain}? Gut check, no wrong answer.

map.CSE: Perfect. {packagePush}For CSE the game is clear: a strong coding profile -- LeetCode practice, real projects on GitHub, internships, hackathons. Let me line up the exact roadmap for your {answers.domain} goal.
map.ECE: Perfect. {packagePush}For ECE, listen carefully: alongside coding basics, VLSI and embedded certifications genuinely matter -- and your college courses (digital design, signals) are NOT just marks, they're your foundation. Let me line up your {answers.domain} roadmap.
map.EEE: Perfect. {packagePush}For EEE the winning combo is: core certifications (MATLAB, power/control tools), taking your college courses seriously, plus basic coding. Let me line up your {answers.domain} roadmap.
map.Civil: Perfect. {packagePush}For Civil, the industry looks for tool certifications -- AutoCAD, STAAD Pro -- and strong fundamentals from your college courses. Site exposure counts too. Let me line up your {answers.domain} roadmap.
map.Mechanical: Perfect. {packagePush}For Mechanical, CAD tool certifications (SolidWorks, Fusion 360), your core college courses, and hands-on projects are what open doors. Let me line up your {answers.domain} roadmap.
